title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Spiral Review
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a domain constant and configuration policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.
Name stable domain values by meaning and keep them near their owning policy.
Use enums, unions, literal types, branded values, or value objects for closed domains.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates constants literals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Constant Tests trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test boundary values and downstream behavior instead of only constant equality.

content.text: For Constant Tests, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Constant Tests precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates constants literals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A constant declaration cannot cause behavioral regressions.

stimulus.title: Constant Tests case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Constant Tests applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test boundary values and downstream behavior instead of only constant equality.
